---
title: "Cricket Data Analysis - Batsmen Performance"
author: "Team CricEval"
date: "`r Sys.Date()`"
output: html_document
---

```{r}
install.packages(c(
  "tidyverse",
  "data.table",
  "ggplot2",
  "corrplot",
  "caret",
  "cluster",
  "factoextra",
  "GGally",
  "reshape2"
))

# Load libraries
library(tidyverse)
library(data.table)
library(ggplot2)
library(corrplot)
library(caret)
library(cluster)
library(factoextra)
library(GGally)
library(reshape2)
library(stringr)
```

```{r}

# Read dataset
df <- read_csv("top10_batsmen_dataset.csv")

# View first rows
head(df)

# Dimensions (rows, columns)
dim(df)

# Structure
str(df)

# Summary stats
summary(df)

# Column names
colnames(df)

# 1. Missing values per column
missing_values <- colSums(is.na(df))
missing_values

# 2. Empty strings in character columns
empty_strings <- sapply(df, function(x) { if (is.character(x)) sum(x == "", na.rm = TRUE) else 0 })
empty_strings

# 3. Duplicate rows
duplicate_count <- sum(duplicated(df))
duplicate_count

# 4. Data types of all columns
data_types <- sapply(df, class)
data_types

# 5. Detect possible mixed/wrong types (numeric stored as character)
mixed_type_check <- sapply(df, function(x) { if (is.character(x)) { suppressWarnings(sum(!is.na(as.numeric(x)))) } else { NA } })
mixed_type_check
```

# 🧹 3. Data Cleaning

```{r}

# 1. Remove duplicate rows
df <- df %>% distinct()

# 2. Trim whitespace from character columns

df <- df %>% mutate(across(where(is.character), str_trim))

# 3. Standardize text casing (lowercase for consistency)

df <- df %>% mutate(across(where(is.character), str_to_lower))

df <- df %>% select(-season)
```

# 🏏 5. Understand the Structure of the Cricket Data

```{r}

# 1. Unique batsmen

num_batsmen <- n_distinct(df$striker) num_batsmen

# 2. Matches, seasons, venues, opponents

num_matches <- n_distinct(df$match_id)
num_seasons <- n_distinct(df$season) num_venues <- n_distinct(df$venue)
num_opponents <- n_distinct(df$bowling_team)

# 3. Understand dataset level (by checking uniqueness)

# Check how many balls per match

df %\>% group_by(match_id) %\>% summarise(total_balls = n()) %\>% head()

# Check balls per batsman

df %\>% group_by(striker) %\>% summarise(balls_faced = n()) %\>% arrange(desc(balls_faced)) %\>% head()

# 4. Identify column types (manual understanding)

colnames(df)

# 5. Decide analysis level

# Ball-level data example

head(df)

# Batsman-level aggregation

batsman_summary <- df %\>% group_by(striker) %\>% summarise( total_runs = sum(runs_off_bat, na.rm = TRUE), balls = n(), strike_rate = (total_runs / balls) * 100 )

head(batsman_summary)

# Match-level summary

match_summary <- df %\>% group_by(match_id) %\>% summarise( total_runs = sum(runs_off_bat, na.rm = TRUE), total_wickets = sum(wicket, na.rm = TRUE) )

head(match_summary)
```

# 📊 7. Univariate EDA

```{r}

# 1. Batsman frequency

df %\>% count(striker, sort = TRUE) %\>% head(10)

# 2. Bowler frequency

df %\>% count(bowler, sort = TRUE) %\>% head(10)

# 3. Venue frequency

df %\>% count(venue, sort = TRUE) %\>% head(10)

# 4. Season frequency

df %\>% count(season, sort = TRUE)

# 5. Batting team frequency

df %\>% count(batting_team, sort = TRUE)

# 6. Bowling team frequency

df %\>% count(bowling_team, sort = TRUE)

# 7. Shot type frequency

df %\>% count(shot_played, sort = TRUE) %\>% head(10)

# 8. Shot direction frequency

df %\>% count(shot_direction, sort = TRUE) %\>% head(10)

# 9. Wicket type frequency

df %\>% count(wicket_type, sort = TRUE)
```

```{r}

# 1. Runs scored per ball

ggplot(df, aes(x = runs_off_bat)) + geom_histogram(bins = 10) + labs(title = "Distribution of Runs per Ball")

# 2. Extras distribution

ggplot(df, aes(x = extras)) + geom_histogram(bins = 10) + labs(title = "Distribution of Extras")

# 3. Balls faced (using aggregated data)

batsman_stats <- df %\>% group_by(striker) %\>% summarise( balls = n(), total_runs = sum(runs_off_bat, na.rm = TRUE), strike_rate = (total_runs / balls) * 100 )

# Balls faced distribution

ggplot(batsman_stats, aes(x = balls)) + geom_histogram(bins = 20) + labs(title = "Distribution of Balls Faced")

# 4. Total runs distribution

ggplot(batsman_stats, aes(x = total_runs)) + geom_histogram(bins = 20) + labs(title = "Distribution of Total Runs")

# 5. Strike rate distribution

ggplot(batsman_stats, aes(x = strike_rate)) + geom_histogram(bins = 20) + labs(title = "Distribution of Strike Rate")
```

# 🔗 8. Bivariate EDA

```{r}

# Create batsman summary (reuse this everywhere)

batsman_stats <- df %\>% group_by(striker) %\>% summarise( total_runs = sum(runs_off_bat, na.rm = TRUE), balls = n(), strike_rate = (total_runs / balls) * 100 ) %\>% filter(balls > 50) # remove very small samples

# 1. Compare runs by batsman (Top 10)

batsman_stats %\>% arrange(desc(total_runs)) %\>% head(10) %\>% ggplot(aes(x = reorder(striker, total_runs), y = total_runs)) + geom_col() + coord_flip() + labs(title = "Top 10 Batsmen by Total Runs")

# 2. Compare strike rate by batsman

batsman_stats %\>% arrange(desc(strike_rate)) %\>% head(10) %\>% ggplot(aes(x = reorder(striker, strike_rate), y = strike_rate)) + geom_col() + coord_flip() + labs(title = "Top 10 Batsmen by Strike Rate")

# 3. Performance by venue

venue_stats <- df %\>% group_by(venue) %\>% summarise(avg_runs = mean(runs_off_bat, na.rm = TRUE))

ggplot(venue_stats, aes(x = reorder(venue, avg_runs), y = avg_runs)) + geom_col() + coord_flip() + labs(title = "Average Runs per Ball by Venue")

# 4. Performance by opponent (bowling team)

opponent_stats <- df %\>% group_by(bowling_team) %\>% summarise(avg_runs = mean(runs_off_bat, na.rm = TRUE))

ggplot(opponent_stats, aes(x = reorder(bowling_team, avg_runs), y = avg_runs)) + geom_col() + coord_flip() + labs(title = "Performance vs Opponent")

# 5. Performance by season

season_stats <- df %\>% group_by(season) %\>% summarise(avg_runs = mean(runs_off_bat, na.rm = TRUE))

ggplot(season_stats, aes(x = season, y = avg_runs)) + geom_line() + geom_point() + labs(title = "Performance Across Seasons")

# 6. Performance by batting style

bat_style_stats <- df %\>% group_by(`batting style_striker`) %\>% summarise(avg_runs = mean(runs_off_bat, na.rm = TRUE))

ggplot(bat_style_stats, aes(x = `batting style_striker`, y = avg_runs)) + geom_col() + labs(title = "Performance by Batting Style")

# 7. Performance by bowling style

bowl_style_stats <- df %\>% group_by(`bowling style_bowler`) %\>% summarise(avg_runs = mean(runs_off_bat, na.rm = TRUE))

ggplot(bowl_style_stats, aes(x = `bowling style_bowler`, y = avg_runs)) + geom_col() + labs(title = "Performance vs Bowling Style")

# 8. Shot type vs runs

shot_stats <- df %\>% group_by(shot_played) %\>% summarise(avg_runs = mean(runs_off_bat, na.rm = TRUE))

ggplot(shot_stats, aes(x = reorder(shot_played, avg_runs), y = avg_runs)) + geom_col() + coord_flip() + labs(title = "Runs by Shot Type")

# 9. Shot direction vs runs

direction_stats <- df %\>% group_by(shot_direction) %\>% summarise(avg_runs = mean(runs_off_bat, na.rm = TRUE))
ggplot(direction_stats, aes(x = reorder(shot_direction, avg_runs), y = avg_runs)) + geom_col() + coord_flip() + labs(title = "Runs by Shot Direction")
```

```{r}
# Visualisation
## Bar Chart for Category Counts
# Batsman frequency (Stacked bar chart)
ggplot(df, aes(x = striker, fill = batting_team)) +
  geom_bar() +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 90)) +
  labs(
    title = "Ball Count per Batsman",
    x = "Batsman",
    y = "Ball Count",
    fill = "Batting Team"
  )
# From this plot, we can
# - Find who played the most balls
# - Identify multi-team players
# - Identify role of the batsman
#   - High count -> anchor player
#   - Low count -> finisher / tail-ender

# Batting team
ggplot(df %>% count(batting_team),
       aes(x = reorder(batting_team, n), y = n, fill = batting_team)) +
  geom_bar(stat="identity") +
  coord_flip() +
  labs(
    title = "Balls Faced by Each Team",
    x = "Balls",
    y = "Batting Team",
    fill = "Batting Team"
  )
# From this plot, we can
# - Identify which team appears most in the dataset
# - Identify dataset imbalance

# geom_bar() is used to create bar charts
```

```{r}
# Runs distribution
ggplot(df, aes(x = runs_off_bat)) +
  geom_histogram(bins = 20, fill='blue') +
  labs(
    title = "Distribution of Runs per Ball",
    x = "Runs off bat",
    y = "Count"
  )

# Extras distribution
ggplot(df, aes(x = extras)) +
  geom_histogram(bins = 20, fill="green") +
  labs(
    title = "Distribution of Extras",
    x = "Extra",
    y = "Count"
  )
```

```{r}
ggplot(df, aes(x = striker, y = runs_off_bat)) +
  geom_violin(fill = "lightblue") +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 90)) +
  ggtitle("Batsman Performance")
# Width of violin
# - Wider → more frequent values
# - Narrow → rare values
# - Example
#   - Wide at 0 → many dot balls
#   - Wide at 4 → many boundaries
```

```{r}
# Runs vs extras
ggplot(df, aes(x = extras, y = runs_off_bat)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.5) +
  labs(
    title = "Runs vs Extra per Ball",
    x = "Extra run team got",
    y = "Runs batsman scored on that ball"
  )
# This helps us learn about the different types of extras
# - No balls (with six, with four, dot ball, etc)
# - Wide (...)
# - byes (...)

# Balls faced vs runs (after aggregation)
batsman_df <- df %>%
  group_by(striker) %>%
  summarise(
    total_runs = sum(runs_off_bat),
    balls = n()
  )
```

```{r}
# Check missing values
colSums(is.na(df))

# Notice that all NA values are expected to be there
```

```{r}
# Outlier Detection
batsman_df <- df %>%
  group_by(striker) %>%
  summarise(
    total_runs = sum(runs_off_bat),
    balls = n(),
    strike_rate = (total_runs / balls) * 100
  )
```

```{r}
detect_outliers <- function(x) {
  Q1 <- quantile(x, 0.25)
  Q3 <- quantile(x, 0.75)
  IQR <- Q3 - Q1
  
  lower <- Q1 - 1.5 * IQR
  upper <- Q3 + 1.5 * IQR
  
  return(x < lower | x > upper)
}

batsman_df <- batsman_df %>%
  mutate(
    outlier_sr = detect_outliers(strike_rate),
    outlier_runs = detect_outliers(total_runs),
    outlier_balls = detect_outliers(balls)
  )

outliers_df <- batsman_df %>%
  filter(outlier_sr | outlier_runs | outlier_balls)

outliers_df
```

```{r}
# runs_off_bat
ggplot(df, aes(y = runs_off_bat)) +
  geom_boxplot(fill = "skyblue") +
  ggtitle("Runs per Ball per Batsman")

# strike rate
ggplot(batsman_df, aes(y = strike_rate)) +
  geom_boxplot(fill = "lightgreen") +
  ggtitle("Strike Rate per Batsman")

# balls faced
ggplot(batsman_df, aes(y = balls)) +
  geom_boxplot(fill = "orange") +
  ggtitle("Balls Faced per Batsman")

# total runs
ggplot(batsman_df, aes(y = total_runs)) +
  geom_boxplot(fill = "pink") +
  ggtitle("Total Runs per Batsman")
```

### One Hot Encoding

#### Venue Grouping

-   Group venues which are very less frequent into a single class "Others"

```{r}
top_venues <- df %>%
  count(venue, sort = TRUE) %>%
  slice(1:10) %>%
  pull(venue)
top_venues

df$venue_group <- ifelse(df$venue %in% top_venues, df$venue, "Others")

# One Hot Encoding
venue_encoded <- model.matrix(~ venue_group - 1, data = df)

# Drop original
df <- df %>% select(-venue, -venue_group)

# Combine
df <- cbind(df, venue_encoded)
head(df)
```

```{r}
encoded_df <- model.matrix(
  ~ batting_team + bowling_team + shot_played + shot_direction - 1,
  data = df_
)

encoded_df <- as.data.frame(encoded_df)
head(encoded_df)
```

```{r}
# No column to do Label Encoding as there is no column with ordinal values
```

```{r}
str(encoded_df)
```
